generated_oml:
// Data Transmitted ----------------------------------------------
instance RawLiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance MotorStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStatusSensor
]

instance SteeringStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringSensor
]

instance ServoPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoPositionSensor
]

// Sensing Components ----------------------------------------------
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Lidar sensor emitting raw point clouds for the autonomous vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:producedData RawLiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor status sensor emitting current motor metrics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering sensor emitting steering angle data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance ServoPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Servo position sensor emitting servo joint positions."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoPositionDataTransmitted
]

// Data Entities ----------------------------------------------
instance RawLiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud frames received from the physical twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance MotorStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current motor metrics emitted from the physical twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCComputationEnabler
]

instance SteeringStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current steering angle metrics emitted from the physical twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCComputationEnabler
]

instance ServoPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current servo joint positions emitted from the physical twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ServoPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCComputationEnabler
]

instance PointClusterData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "2D point clusters derived from LiDAR frames."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCComputationEnabler
]

instance UserInterfaceRemoteCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data stream of user interface commands for remote management."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RemoteCommandEnabler
]

// Insights ----------------------------------------------
instance VehicleStateVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of real‑time vehicle state metrics."
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into detected object tracks derived from LiDAR clustering."
]

instance DrivingPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into projected driving path based on current vehicle trajectory."
]

instance LeadVehicleDistanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight computing lead vehicle distance for ACC."
]

// Actions ----------------------------------------------
instance ComputeDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action computing desired speed based on ACC logic."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to enable or disable the ACC system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to set a desired speed value."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to trigger emergency braking command."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// Services ----------------------------------------------
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real‑time vehicle state data, projected driving paths, and detected object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateVisualization
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides DrivingPathInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service running ACC system computation and sending computed speed command back to vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputeDesiredSpeedAction
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDistanceInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speeds, and issue emergency braking commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction
    DTDFVocab:provides SetDesiredSpeedAction
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyBrakeAction
]

// Enablers ----------------------------------------------
instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 component converting raw LiDAR frames into 2D point clusters, tracks and projected driving path."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RawLiDARPointCloudData
]

instance ACCComputationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 component running ACC logic using vehicle dynamics and perception data."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorStatusData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SteeringStatusData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointClusterData
]

instance RemoteCommandEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 service handling remote operator commands such as enable ACC, set speed, emergency brake."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UserInterfaceRemoteCommandData
]

// Acting Components ----------------------------------------------
instance SpeedCommandActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator sending computed speed command from digital twin to vehicle’s ECU."
]

// User Interface Component ----------------------------------------------
instance UserInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User interface providing visualization and control panel interactions."
]

// Environments ----------------------------------------------
instance PhysicalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiDARSensor
    base:contains MotorStatusSensor
    base:contains SteeringSensor
    base:contains ServoPositionSensor
    base:contains SpeedCommandActuator
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PointCloudProcessingEnabler
    base:contains ACCComputationEnabler
    base:contains RemoteCommandEnabler
    base:contains DataMonitoringService
    base:contains ComputationOffloadingService
    base:contains RemoteManagementService
]

instance UserInterfaceEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains UserInterface
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Digital twin sends control commands to the physical twin through the Actuation FE. Command types include motor actuation (throttle/brake), steering servo actuation, and other actuator position commands. The document indicates that these commands are communicated via ROS2 messages over the internal network; however, specific serialization formats, authentication mechanisms, conflict resolution strategies, and feedback confirmation procedures are not described."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "All vehicle‑side functional entities rely on a 100 ms clock‑synchronisation accuracy mandated by safety‑critical vehicular standards. ROS 2 nodes within each Functional Entity timestamp their messages using DDS time stamps that are synchronised via a common network time protocol (e.g., IEEE 1588 Precision Time Protocol) to achieve sub‑100 ms jitter. The Digital Twin’s virtual entities maintain a 100 ms temporal granularity, matching the physical twin’s sensor update rates, and the system tolerates computation‑offload latencies up to 100 ms before data propagation back to the vehicle."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration follows ISO 23247 Reference Architecture, mapping each Functional Entity (FE) to an individual ROS 2 package that contains one or more ROS 2 nodes. The User Entity (visualisation, control panel) is implemented as a ROS 2 launch file that starts corresponding nodes; the Digital Twin Entity contains packages for data storage, analytics, and control; the Physical Twin Entity hosts sensor‑feedback, point‑cloud clustering, and tracking packages. Each package communicates over DDS using QoS profiles that guarantee real‑time constraints. The system’s resource management strategy assigns compute‑heavy tasks (e.g., point‑cloud clustering) to high‑performance edge nodes while lightweight node interfaces run on the vehicle’s in‑vehicle network. Coordination among entities is achieved via ROS 2 topics, services, and actions, with a central ROS 2 master node ensuring single‑source time stamps and message routing."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DTS architecture combines a ROS2 internal network (typically using DDS over UDP) for inter‑component communication and a Wi‑Fi interface employing UDP for inter‑entity links between physical, digital, and user entities. The document does not specify concrete bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, or security protocols for either domain."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system aligns with ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing, Part 2 (Reference Architecture), adopting an entity‑based architecture where observable manufacturing elements are represented by a Digital Twin Entity, data is collected and controlled by a Data Collection and Device Control Entity, and users interact via a User Entity. Functional Entities handle specific tasks such as actuation, data transformation, and user interfacing, though the standard does not prescribe implementation details. OPC Unified Architecture is cited as an example implementation protocol, facilitating interoperability. Compliance thus relies on adherence to ISO 23247 Part 2 specifications and enables interoperability through the established entity relationships and OPC UA compatibility. "
]
